entries.statement: Ability to design and implement AI risk governance frameworks calibrated to organizational risk tolerance and governance objectives.
Ability to develop and implement data and AI governance policies and procedures that operationalize organizational governance frameworks.
Ability to assess and comprehensively inventory organizational data assets, data flows, and technology resources to inform governance decisions.
Ability to coordinate data sharing, integration, and consolidation initiatives across organizational units and external partners.
Ability to monitor and control AI systems' operational performance against established governance standards, policies, and risk thresholds.
Ability to communicate complex data governance and AI governance strategies, capabilities, and risk profiles to executive stakeholders and diverse organizational audiences.
Ability to conduct end-to-end AI system governance from business case definition through deployment, including business value assessment, risk evaluation, and deployment decision-making.
Ability to establish and oversee human oversight mechanisms and transparency governance requirements for AI systems to ensure human control and explainability.
Ability to identify and govern risks arising from AI supply chains, third-party AI components, and external dependencies within AI systems.
Ability to assess organizational readiness, capability gaps, and workforce development needs for executing data governance and AI governance functions.
Ability to oversee and govern data architecture decisions, integration patterns, and technology platform choices to ensure alignment with governance and organizational objectives.
Ability to prioritize data and AI governance investments, resource allocations, and initiatives under competing organizational demands and resource constraints.
Ability to assess and govern interactions, dependencies, and trade-offs among multiple concurrent AI systems to ensure balanced risk management and organizational optimization.
